敌.origin: 舌 plus the tap radical 攵 — the one you strike against is your enemy.

sentence.en: They defeated the enemy.

敏.origin: 每 plus the tap radical 攵 — quick to act, sharp and nimble.

sentence.en: The cat moves very nimbly.

救.origin: 求 (to beg) plus the tap radical 攵 — to rush in and help the one who begs.

sentence.en: The firefighter went to save people.

敖.origin: An old character meaning to wander and play freely about.

sentence.en: There is an ovoo on the grassland.

敛.origin: 佥 plus the tap radical 攵 — to gather things in and hold them together.

sentence.en: His smile faded.

敞.origin: 尚 plus the tap radical 攵 — wide open and spacious.

sentence.en: This classroom is very spacious.

散.origin: A hand 攵 scattering things apart so they break up and spread.

sentence.en: After dinner we go for a walk.

敲.origin: 高 plus the strike radical 攴 — to knock and tap on something.

sentence.en: Someone is knocking at the door.

整.origin: 敕 over 正 (correct) — to put things in correct, neat order.

sentence.en: His room is very tidy.

敷.origin: 旉 plus the tap radical 攵 — the hand spreads and applies something on.

sentence.en: The nurse applied medicine to him.

文.origin: A picture of crossing lines like a tattoo, standing for writing and language.

sentence.en: I like the Chinese language class.

斋.origin: 文 over 而 — a quiet study room, or to fast and keep clean of meat.

sentence.en: Grandpa reads in his study.

斑.origin: Markings 文 set between two pieces of jade 王 — spots and stripes.

sentence.en: There are zebras in the zoo.

斜.origin: 余 plus the measuring-cup 斗 — leaning to one side, slanting.

sentence.en: The little path is a bit sloped.

斟.origin: 甚 plus the measuring-cup 斗 — to pour carefully, weighing how much.

sentence.en: He poured tea for the guest.

斤.origin: A picture of an axe head; it became the unit of weight, a catty.

sentence.en: This bag of rice is five kilograms.

斩.origin: 车 plus the axe 斤 — to chop and cut through with an axe.

sentence.en: He cut the rope with one stroke.

施.origin: 方 plus 㐌 — to give out, to put a plan into action.

sentence.en: The workers are doing construction.

旋.origin: A flag 方 with a foot 疋 beneath — turning round and round.

sentence.en: The dancer spins quickly.

旗.origin: The flag radical 方 plus 其 — a banner or flag waving in the wind.

sentence.en: The national flag rises over the field.

旦.origin: The sun 日 rising above the horizon line 一 — dawn, the start of a day.

sentence.en: New Year's Day is a one-day holiday.

旨.origin: A spoon 匕 over a mouth 日 — the aim or main idea, what is meant.

sentence.en: This is our purpose.

旬.origin: A wrap 勹 around the sun 日 — a cycle of ten days.

word.en: first ten days of a month

sentence.en: He will return in the last ten days of the month.

旱.origin: The sun 日 over 干 (dry) — long sun with no rain, a drought.

sentence.en: It is very dry this year.

旷.origin: The sun 日 plus 广 (wide) — a wide, open plain under the sky.

sentence.en: The grassland is very wide and open.

旺.origin: The sun 日 plus 王 — bright and full of life, flourishing.

sentence.en: The flowers are growing vigorously.

昂.origin: The sun 日 over 卬 — to lift the head high and rise up.

sentence.en: He walks with his head held high.

昆.origin: The sun 日 over 比 (two side by side) — brothers, and small crawling things.

sentence.en: An ant is a kind of insect.

昌.origin: A sun 日 over another sun 曰 — bright and flourishing.

sentence.en: The city is growing more and more prosperous.

昏.origin: 氏 over the sun 日 — the sun sinking low, dusk and dimness.

sentence.en: At dusk the sky turned red.

易.origin: An old picture once read as a lizard; it means easy and to change.

sentence.en: This question is very easy.

映.origin: The sun 日 plus 央 — sunlight shining on something and reflecting back.

sentence.en: The lake reflects the blue sky.

昼.origin: 尺 over 旦 — when the sun is up high; the daytime.

sentence.en: He works day and night without stopping.

晃.origin: The sun 日 over 光 (light) — bright and dazzling, or to sway.

sentence.en: The little boat sways on the water.

晋.origin: 亚 over the sun 日 — to move forward and advance.

word.en: to advance a level, to be promoted

sentence.en: He advanced to the next level smoothly.

晌.origin: The sun 日 plus 向 — the time when the sun is high; midday.

sentence.en: At noon the sun is very strong.

晒.origin: The sun 日 plus 西 — to leave things in the sun to dry.

sentence.en: Grandma dries clothes in the yard.

晕.origin: The sun 日 over 军 — feeling foggy and dizzy.

sentence.en: I feel a little dizzy.

普.origin: 並 (together) over the sun 日 — everyone under the sun; wide and general.

sentence.en: This is an ordinary day.

晾.origin: The sun 日 plus 京 — to hang things out in the air to dry.

word.en: to hang out clothes to dry

sentence.en: Mom hangs clothes to dry on the balcony.

暂.origin: 斩 over the sun 日 — lasting only a short time; temporary.

sentence.en: I'm not leaving for now.

暴.origin: The sun 日 above hands 共 — sudden and fierce, violent.

sentence.en: There was a rainstorm last night.

曰.origin: A mouth 口 with a line 一 inside, showing breath — to speak, to say.

sentence.en: The ancients said: reading is good.

曹.origin: An old word for a group of officials; also a common family name.

sentence.en: His surname is Cao.

曾.origin: A picture of a steamer letting off steam; it came to mean once, formerly.

sentence.en: I have once been to Beijing.

替.origin: One man 夫 standing in for another over 日 — to replace or stand in.

sentence.en: I'll carry the books for you.

望.origin: A person 王 gazing up at the moon 月 — to look far off and to hope.

sentence.en: I hope it will be sunny tomorrow.

朱.origin: A line 一 marking the red sap inside a tree 木 — the color vermilion.

sentence.en: The gate is vermilion red.

朵.origin: 几 over a tree 木 — a flower blooming on a branch; a measure word for flowers.

sentence.en: There is a flower on the table.

机.origin: The tree/wood radical 木 plus 几 — a thing made of wood; now a machine.

sentence.en: The airplane flies in the sky.
